summary: Top 6 high-probability +EV picks · NBA slate (DET-ORL · CLE-TOR · LAL-HOU) · 4 of 6 are clean +EV vs market

picks.reasoning: Headline pick: clearest market mispricing on the board. Amen's median = 18.4 pts with σ ≈ 8.15, so 19.5 sits comfortably above his 50/50 number. RI's -108 implies 51.9% but the model puts true under prob at 55.5% → +6.9% EV. The market consensus (FanDuel, ProphetX) prices the under near 60% — RI is selling this for ~8 points cheaper than every sharp book. With KD as the offensive hub and Reaves/LeBron eating the LAL possessions, Amen is more of a connector tonight than the Houston scoring engine.

Strong +EV play on the secondary RI line. WCJ p50 = 8.0 reb with σ ≈ 3.96, so 8.5 sits just above the median. RI's -110 implies 52.4% but the model says 55.1% → +5.2% EV. Banchero is the primary rebounder for ORL (p50 = 8.1) and Duren (p50 = 8.85) crashes the boards from the other side, capping WCJ's chance window. Most other books are at the 8.5 number with juice closer to -125 to -135 — RI's -110 is a clean mispriced under.

Solid +EV under with the highest hit rate among +EV plays on the slate. Paolo p50 = 23.0 pts with σ ≈ 9.16. RI's -124 implies 55.4%; model puts true under at 56.5% → +2.1% EV. The line at 24.5 is 1.5 points above his projected median — Detroit's defense isn't elite but Cade-Ausar Thompson can absorb minutes when Paolo sits with foul trouble (his usual problem). Vig is light enough that the small edge clears.

Coin-flip with a tiny edge from RI's near-flat juice. Sengun p50 = 9.35 reb with σ ≈ 4.34, so 9.5 sits just above median. RI's -102 implies 50.5%; model says 51.4% → +1.8% EV. Ayton (p50 = 8.7 reb) is the rim-running counterweight in this game and Smith Jr./Eason both crash. Almost no vig on the under side here — the price is what makes the play, not a strong projection edge.

Highest hit rate on the slate (57.0%). Mobley p50 = 8.7 reb with σ ≈ 4.45 — 9.5 line sits roughly 0.8 above his median. RI's -146 implies 59.4%; model puts true at 57.0% → -2.4% EV (the vig is the cost of safety). Allen is the primary rebounder for CLE (p50 = 7.7) and Poeltl crashes hard for TOR. Mobley plays positional D more than glass-cleaning. If you want a high-prob anchor, this clears 60%+ of the time on a normal workload.

Near-fair price, near-fair model — a clean over to balance the slate's heavy under tilt. Scottie p50 = 7.83 ast with σ ≈ 3.62. RI's -118 implies 54.1%; model says 53.6% → -1.0% EV (basically a vig wash). Barnes is TOR's lead initiator now with Ingram off-ball — he's averaging ~7-8 dimes in his last 10. RJ Barrett's scoring volume gives him plenty of finishers; the assists should keep coming as long as TOR plays him 33+ min.
